session.tool_result:

Unlike regular dispatch, this does not require 8→the erk-plan label — just an OPEN PR. 9→""" 10→ 11→import json 12→from pathlib import Path 13→ 14→import click 15→ 16→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_cmd import load_workflow_config 17→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import ensure_trunk_synced 18→from erk.cli.commands.ref_resolution import resolve_dispatch_ref 19→from erk.cli.constants import DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME 20→from erk_shared.context.helpers import ( 21→ require_branch_manager, 22→ require_context, 23→ require_git, 24→ require_github, 25→ require_repo_root, 26→ require_time, 27→) 28→from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel 29→from erk_shared.gateway.git.remote_ops.types import PushError 30→from erk_shared.gateway.github.parsing import (
... [189 lines omitted] ...
83→ _output_error(output_format, f"PR #{pr_number} not found") 87→ _output_error(output_format, f"PR #{pr_number} is {pr_result.state}, must be OPEN") 98→ _output_error(output_format, "Not in a git repository") 147→ if isinstance(push_result, PushError): 148→ _output_error(output_format, f"Push failed: {push_result.message}") 208→def _output_error(output_format: str, message: str) -> None: 209→ """Output error in the requested format.""" 211→ click.echo(json.dumps({"success": False, "error": message})) 213→ user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + message)
session.assistant:
Now I'll provide you with a comprehensive analysis of the file:
## Overall Structure and Purpose
The file `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/incremental_dispatch.py` is an exec CLI command that dispatches a local plan against an existing PR for remote implementation. Unlike regular dispatch, it does not require the erk-plan label—just an OPEN PR. It commits the plan as impl-context to the PR's branch and triggers the plan-implement workflow.
## Key Functions and Line Numbers
| Function | Line Numbers | Purpose | |----------|--------------|---------| | `incremental_dispatch()` | 62-205 | Main CLI command handler | | `_output_error()` | 208-213 | Helper to output errors in requested format |
## Commit Message Format (Line 128)
The commit message is constructed on line 128 as: ```python message=f"Incremental dispatch for PR #{pr_number}\n\n{plan_content}", ```
This creates a two-part commit message: 1. **First line (subject)**: `Incremental dispatch for PR #{pr_number}` 2. **Second part (body)**: The full `plan_content` (plan markdown) embedded directly
This is the **two-part plan embedding strategy** - the plan itself is embedded in the git commit message body.
## Worktree Interaction/Index Sync Logic (Lines 135-141)
The logic handles the case where the branch is already checked out in a worktree:
```python # If branch is checked out, write files to disk and stage so git status is clean if checked_out_path is not None: for rel_path, content in files.items(): abs_path = checked_out_path / rel_path abs_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) abs_path.write_text(content, encoding="utf-8") git.commit.stage_files(checked_out_path, list(files.keys()), force=True) ```
This process: 1. **Detects if branch is checked out**: Line 105-106 checks `git.worktree.is_branch_checked_out()` 2. **Writes impl-context files to disk**: Lines 137-140 materialize the files (created by `build_impl_context_files()`) 3. **Stages files to git index**: Line 141 calls `git.commit.stage_files()` with `force=True` to ensure the working tree matches the plumbing commit
This ensures git status is clean after the plumbing commit advanced the branch.
## Workflow Dispatch Details (Lines 162-172)
The workflow dispatch uses two key parameters in the inputs dictionary:
| Parameter | Line | Value | Purpose | |-----------|------|-------|---------| | `dispatch_type` | 170 | `"incremental"` | Indicates this is an incremental dispatch (vs. full dispatch) | | `plan_backend` | 169 | `"planned_pr"` | Specifies the plan backend type for the remote implementation workflow |
Additional dispatch inputs (lines 162-172): - `plan_id`: PR number (line 163) - `submitted_by`: GitHub username (line 164) - `plan_title`: PR title (line 165) - `branch_name`: Target branch (line 166) - `pr_number`: PR number as string (line 167) - `base_branch`: Detected trunk branch (line 168) - Plus additional config from `load_workflow_config()` (line 171)
The workflow is triggered on line 175 using `github.trigger_workflow()` with `DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME` and the ref resolved from dispatch options.
